LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THERE BEING NO PROOF OF PROPER NOTICE BY PLAINTIFF THAT SHE WAS CONTESTING THE TENTATIVE RULING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUDAKOV DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 7, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST IMMEDIATELY DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HER OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION AND REPLY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING TO GIVE PLAINTIFF ANOTHER OPPORTUNITY TO CURE THE DEFECT(S) IN HER COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS VLADIMIR RUDAKOV AND COUNTY ADULT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ON APRIL 17, 2013 FAILS TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT RUDAKOV. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO IDENTIFY ANY CONDUCT BY MR. RUDAKOV THAT COULD FORM THE BASIS FOR A CLAIM AGAINST HIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF HEATHER GIAKOVMIS NOTICE OF MOTION TO RECONSIDER "ORDER GRANTING DEFTS SECOND MOTION TO QUASH". DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1008, WHICH REQUIRES THAT MOTIONS FOR RECONSIDERATION BE FILED WITHIN TEN DAYS AFTER SERVICE UPON THE PARTY OF WRITTEN NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER. PLAINTIFF MUST RE-SERVE DEFENDANT WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT AND FILE HER PROOF OF SERVICE WITH THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUDAKOV'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FROM ENTRY OF ORDER, PURSUANT TO CCP 430.10(E), FOR FAILURE TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT RUDAKOV. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE THE LEGAL WRONG SHE HAS SUFFERED, DEFENDANT RUDAKOV'S ROLE IN CAUSING THAT WRONG, AND THE RELIEF SHE IS SEEKING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bradley A. Russi, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3964, for defendant and moving party;
Heather Giakovmis, plaintiff appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling to give plaintiff another opportunity to cure the defect(s) in her complaint. DEFENDANTS VLADIMIR RUDAKOV and COUNTY ADULT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend from notice of entry of order. No opposition filed. The Amended Complaint filed by Plaintiff on April 17, 2013 fails to allege sufficient facts to state a cause of action against Defendant Rudakov. Plaintiff fails to identify any conduct by Mr. Rudakov that could form the basis for a claim against him. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: HEATHER GIAKOVMIS, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
BRADLEY RUSSI, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUDAKOV.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUDAKOV'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained with 20 days leave to amend from entry of order, pursuant to CCP 430.10(e), for failure to allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action against Defendant Rudakov. Plaintiff is granted leave to file an amended complaint to allege the legal wrong she has suffered, Defendant Rudakov's role in causing that wrong, and the relief she is seeking. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion To Reconsider "Order Granting Defts Second Motion To Quash"
Off Calendar May-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Heather Giakovmis, Plaintiff and moving party in Pro Per
Michael A. Gerchow, DCA, from the Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3936, for defendant and opposing party

Discussed and The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF HEATHER GIAKOVMIS Notice Of Motion To Reconsider "Order Granting Defts Second Motion To Quash" IS Denied. The motion is untimely under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, which requires that motions for reconsideration be filed within ten days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order. Plaintiff must re-serve Defendant with the summons and complaint and file her proof of service with the Court. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
